differentials.name: neural placode = reddish neural tissue in the middle of back made up of open spinal cord
differentials.raw: neural placode = reddish neural tissue in the middle of back made up of open spinal cord

differentials.name: normal skin / cutaneous abnormality: pigmented nevus, abnormal distribution of hair, skin dimple, angioma, lipoma
differentials.raw: normal skin / cutaneous abnormality: pigmented nevus, abnormal distribution of hair, skin dimple, angioma, lipoma

differentials.name: MS-AFP (≥ 2.5 S.D. over mean) permits detection in 80% (2-5% PPV) if defect not covered by full skin thickness Recurrence rate:   3-7% chance of NTD with previously affected sibling / in fetus of affected parent
differentials.raw: MS-AFP (≥ 2.5 S.D. over mean) permits detection in 80% (2-5% PPV) if defect not covered by full skin thickness Recurrence rate:   3-7% chance of NTD with previously affected sibling / in fetus of affected parent

differentials.raw: Hydrocephalus (70-90%): requiring ventriculoperitoneal shunt in 90% ◊  25% of patients with hydrocephalus have spina bifida!

differentials.raw: Congenital / acquired kyphoscoliosis (90%)

differentials.raw: Vertebral anomalies: vertebral body fusion, hemivertebrae, cleft vertebrae, butterfly vertebrae

differentials.name: Hemimyelocele (10%) = two hemicords in separate dural tubes separated by fibrous / bony spur: one hemicord with myelomeningocele on one side of midline, one hemicord normal / with smaller myelomeningocele at a lower level
differentials.raw: Hemimyelocele (10%) = two hemicords in separate dural tubes separated by fibrous / bony spur: one hemicord with myelomeningocele on one side of midline, one hemicord normal / with smaller myelomeningocele at a lower level

differentials.raw: back pain, numbness in lower limbs, headache ← neurologic compromise / meningitis / rupture

context: =  sac covered by leptomeninges containing CSF + variable amount of neural tissue; herniated through a defect in the posterior / anterior elements of spine Prevalence:   1÷1,000-2,000 births (in Great Britain 1÷200 births); twice as common in infants of mothers > 35 years of age; Caucasians > Blacks > Orientals; most common congenital anomaly of CNS Etiology:   localized defect of closure of caudal neuropore (usually closed by 28 days); persistence of neural placode causes derangement in the development of mesenchymal + ectodermal structures Associated with: Location: (a)  cranial meningocele
